title: alchemilla vulgaris var. heterophylla
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. heterophylla, also known as Lady's Mantle, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Rosaceae, characterized by its rosettes of rounded leaves and delicate white or pink flowers. The medicinal properties of Lady's Mantle are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris var. heterophylla include the regulation of menstrual cycles, relief from menopausal symptoms, and the treatment of digestive disorders, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. Herbal preparations of Lady's Mantle can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote women's health and well-being, and to soothe digestive issues.
